title: Request Lines, Methods, Target Forms, Versions, and Routing Inputs · Execution Trace: Method Token Policy
summary: A parser uses Fields on the request line, normalizes targets before validation, treats every target as origin-form, and accepts invalid CONNECT authorities and absolute-form credentials. Planned artifact: Add and verify the method token policy increment in strict request-line and request-target parser. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Recognize any valid method token, preserve case, then apply supported-method policy separately.
Parse method, one-space delimiters, request target, HTTP version, and CRLF without accepting ambiguous extra components.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Predict Method Token Policy · Theory Method Token Policy
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory protocol team handles case 8388 in a archival client parsing incomplete close-delimited responses. Build deterministic Go evidence to select the request-target form without normalizing away the raw routing boundary; preserve this operating constraint: all attacker-controlled counts must be validated before allocation or slicing; then change certificate identity while ALPN stays valid and reject the connection. Browser code models bounded byte and state decisions only. Sockets, listeners, live TLS, proxies, packet capture, fuzz campaigns, races, load, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Method Token Policy contract for the supplied data.

steps.title: Defend the Method Token Policy model · Theory Method Token Policy
steps.instruction:

Build deterministic Go evidence to select the request-target form without normalizing away the raw routing boundary; preserve this operating constraint: one connection has exactly one read owner and one terminal close owner; then change certificate identity while ALPN stays valid and reject the connection. Browser code models bounded byte and state decisions only. Sockets, listeners, live TLS, proxies, packet capture, fuzz campaigns, races, load, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.
